event_blocks.summary: On the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, featuring a star-themed backdrop and dynamic blue and purple lighting, the group MAYYAS, clad in matching black crop tops, long skirts, wigs, and ornate, bejeweled face veils, entered to a greeting from a female judge who inquired about their identity. A MAYYAS performer confidently introduced them as 'the Mayyas,' explaining the name's meaning as 'the proud walk of a lioness' and highlighting the challenges faced by female Arab dancers. When asked about their planned act, the performer declared they would 'hypnotize' the judges, eliciting an audible 'Oh!' from a male judge and immediate audience cheers. Ethereal, suspenseful music with bell-like sounds and strings began to build as a striking visual illusion unfolded: a central performer stood still while multiple arms emerged from behind her, creating the appearance of a multi-armed figure. These arms moved in synchronized, fluid motions, enhanced by dynamic lighting on the reflective floor. The judges, including the bald man in sunglasses and the woman in the sparkly top, watched with visible awe and surprise, while the audience gasped and cheered loudly. The illusion was maintained throughout, with intricate gestures culminating in a powerful, unified pose with arms raised and hands clasped, all set to dramatic, building orchestral music and continuous, enthusiastic audience applause. The performance transitioned into a towering, multi-layered human structure with arms reaching skyward in a symmetrical starburst pattern, prompting a loud 'Wow!' from the bald man in sunglasses and further audience applause. The group then formed arches before concluding in a unified, synchronized pose with arms raised, leaving the judges in awe amidst continued audience cheers and driving percussive music with intense, chant-like vocalizations. The woman in the sparkly top was particularly animated, her mouth agape and eyes wide, and was later heard saying, 'There are no words to explain to...' as the segment ended.

judging panel.description: A raised platform with three judges seated behind a transparent desk, each with a red Sonic-branded cup, reacting to the performance with expressions of surprise and intrigue, including the woman in sparkly top who verbally expresses her amazement, while male voices from the panel or audience exclaim "Wow!"

woman in sparkly top.description: A female judge with long wavy hair, wearing a sparkly, embellished top, seated at the judging panel, reacting with visible amazement, her mouth agape and eyes wide throughout the performance, and later heard exclaiming, "There are no words to explain to..."

central performer.description: The lead performer in the illusion, standing center stage, wearing the same black outfit and face veil as the group, serving as the focal point from which multiple arms emerge to create a multi-armed visual effect, performing to intense, rhythmic, Middle Eastern-inspired instrumental music.

bald man in sunglasses.description: A male judge with a bald head and sunglasses, seated at the judging panel, observing the performance with a serious expression that shifts to astonishment as the illusion unfolds.

MAYYAS.description: A group of female performers dressed in identical black crop tops and long black skirts, wearing long black wigs with bangs and ornate, bejeweled face veils that cover their lower faces, performing a synchronized illusion act with multiple arms extending from behind one central performer, all to the accompaniment of intense, rhythmic, Middle Eastern-inspired instrumental music.

event_blocks.summary: On the America's Got Talent stage, bathed in dynamic blue and purple lighting with a glowing star backdrop, the MAYYAS, clad in identical black crop tops, long skirts, wigs, and ornate face veils, stood in tight formation. The bald man in sunglasses (Simon Cowell) commended their performance, noting "what we were feeling over here," and emphasized their act would "really, really will make a difference... in so many positive ways," calling it "arguably the best dance act we're likely to have ever seen." A MAYYAS member softly responded, "Thank you." The judging panel, including the woman in a sparkly top, observed with expressions transitioning from curiosity to astonishment as the MAYYAS executed synchronized movements and an illusion of multiple arms. The woman in the sparkly top, speaking emotionally, declared, "I would be so honored to empower you even more in this journey because you deserve it," and "And I want to be part of this!" In response, the central performer raised her arms, prompting the group to erupt in celebration with hands raised in triumph. The audience's cheers intensified as the MAYYAS began singing a powerful, emotional song with harmonies, repeating "It's alright to be lost sometimes," while golden confetti rained down. The judges displayed visible amazement, the woman in the sparkly top with her mouth agape and eyes wide, and Simon clapped enthusiastically. The MAYYAS embraced joyfully, their faces beaming beneath their veils, as the audience and judges applauded wildly. The camera panned across the jubilant stage, highlighting the electric energy, the 'America's Got Talent' logo glowing amidst the confetti and sparkling lights.

event_blocks.summary: The MAYYAS delivered a mesmerizing performance on the America's Got Talent stage, featuring a central performer with multiple arms extending from behind her, while the group, clad in matching black crop tops, long skirts, black wigs, and bejeweled face veils, executed synchronized movements to enthusiastic audience cheers and celebratory music. As golden confetti rained down, the judging panel reacted with astonishment; the woman in a sparkly top exclaimed, "Oh my God, that was so amazing!", and the bald man in sunglasses declared, "Amazing!" He then announced MAYYAS would advance straight to the live show, prompting an eruption of cheers. The scene shifted to V.UNBEATABLE, a group in red outfits with studded belts, standing in formation under vibrant blue and purple lighting. The bald man in sunglasses remarked, "They keep coming!" A female judge, the woman in a colorful jacket, inquired about their group name and origin, to which a male performer from V.UNBEATABLE, speaking with a slight accent, introduced them as "V.UNBEATABLE" from "Mumbai, India." When asked about life in Mumbai, the male performer became emotional describing the hardships of living in a slum without proper water, apologizing as somber background music began. Later, the woman in a colorful jacket asked a V.UNBEATABLE member about their aspirations if they won, and a performer responded, "Uh, we want the world to know who is V.UNBEATABLE." V.UNBEATABLE then launched into a high-energy acrobatic routine on the America's Got Talent stage, under dynamic blue and purple lighting, with a driving, rhythmic musical track featuring energetic Hindi-like vocals. They executed synchronized flips, jumps, human pyramids, and mid-air catches, later incorporating golden poles for dynamic spins and lifts, all met with visible amazement from the judges, including the woman in a sparkly top with her mouth agape and the man in a white shirt leaning forward in awe. event_blocks.summary: The America's Got Talent stage, initially dark and illuminated by neon outlines of performers in black and blue, transformed with the introduction of ZURCAROH. The group, dressed in earth-toned, tribal-inspired costumes, delivered a high-energy acrobatic and gymnastic routine set to intense, rhythmic tribal chanting and percussive music. Performers executed complex formations, dynamic lifts, handstands, splits, mid-air flips, and towering human pyramids, culminating in a female acrobat suspended in a handstand split. Fire torches were introduced, adding a theatrical element. event_blocks.summary: On the America's Got Talent stage, ZURCAROH, clad in earth-toned, tribal costumes, captivated the judging panel, including Simon, Heidi, Mel B, and Howie, with a synchronized acrobatic and dance routine. Heidi, in a purple dress, exclaimed, "Go crazy that you know what I have to do now!" just before a buzzer sounded and golden confetti rained down as she pressed the Golden Buzzer, prompting the crowd to erupt in cheers. A female voice sang, "This is brave, this is bruised, this is who I'm meant to be, this is me," transitioning into an upbeat pop song as the group embraced amidst the confetti. Howie announced, "Congratulations! You are going straight to the live!" to applause, with the on-screen text '@Zurcaroh #GoldenBuzzer' appearing. The scene then shifted to URBANCREW, male dancers in bright red tracksuits, performing a high-energy routine on the same stage, featuring a star backdrop and dynamic lighting. The audience cheered as on-screen text '5 URBANCREW TALENT RECAP' appeared. Simon greeted them, and when asked who they were, a member proudly declared, "We are the Urban Crew! Flash up the South from the Philippines!" The stage, lit with vibrant blue and purple lighting, saw the judging panel, including Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie, each with a red Sonic-branded cup, observing URBANCREW. When Simon asked what they do, a lead member with a Filipino accent explained, "dancing and break dancing and acrobat," leading to Howie's excited "All right!" When asked why AGT, the member passionately stated it's "the biggest stage" and a "big contribution for our beloved countries, Philippines!" Confidently asserting they could win because "we work hard for this," they called it a "once in a lifetime opportunity." Sofia playfully asked if they had "more fun now that you're blonde?" to which the performer enthusiastically replied, "Yes, ma'am!" Simon noted, "I can tell they just want to perform," and Sofia added, "They're ready. You're itching for this." Simon encouraged, "So let's do it. Good luck," and the URBANCREW member thanked him amidst swelling cheers. The performance segment began with an electronic beat and a male voice asking, "People, are you ready?" URBANCREW launched into a high-energy, synchronized, acrobatic routine featuring flips, jumps, and dynamic formations to a driving electronic dance track. Sofia reacted with "Oh!" and "Oh no!" as one dancer was lifted "so high!" The audience cheered throughout, with Sofia exclaiming, "Come on!" and "Look at that!" The reflective floor mirrored the dancers' movements under vibrant lights, culminating in a final synchronized pose amidst loud cheers and applause. The judges, including Simon and Sofia, clapped enthusiastically as the on-screen text '5 URBANCREW TALENT RECAP' appeared.

event_blocks.summary: On the America's Got Talent stage, illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lighting, the group Diavolo, clad in matching white, form-fitting outfits, executed a high-energy acrobatic routine on a large, illuminated semi-circular geometric prop, performing synchronized flips, lifts, and dynamic movements, including a dramatic mid-air suspension. The audience, visibly excited, clapped and cheered amidst intense, dramatic instrumental music, while the judges—Simon, Heidi, Mel B, and Howie—watched with astonishment, mouths agape. As the music built to a crescendo, the audience erupted in roaring cheers and applause, punctuated by a distinct 'BZZZZZZZ' sound as all four judges pressed their buzzers in unison and stood to applaud. Simon remarked, "I kind of feel we need to bring Jacques out," while Mel B exclaimed, "There's somebody out there. Go, go, go, go, go!" Howie added, "Uh-oh!", followed by Heidi's affirmation, "Yeah!" The performers concluded with a powerful final pose amidst continued audience 'WOOOO!'s and applause. Howie reflected, "You know, when I see people dance or do acrobatics, I constantly say as a judge, I want you to take it to the next level," but then praised Diavolo, "And you guys took it beyond the next level. I have never seen anything quite like this in my life." The central performer explained, "it's those dancers that actually create the pieces, which is a beautiful thing." Heidi described the act as "something different," "dangerous looking," and praised the "timing," noting she thought "someone is getting squashed under this thing" and "people are flying off of it," concluding, "it was incredible. Wow!" Mel B found it "so exciting" but "too short," wishing it "needed to be longer, much, much, much longer," and wanting to "see it again and again and again." Simon praised the act as "really, really clever" and "something I will remember," calling it "incredible" and offering "congratulations." As the audience cheered and applauded with swelling music, Simon enthusiastically replied "Oh, yes!" to Howie's "Okay, right, Howie, yes or no?" The segment concluded with Diavolo bowing, the judges continuing to applaud, and the America's Got Talent logo displayed above the stage, with on-screen text '@Diavolo #AGT' and '8 DIAVOLO TALENT RECAP' visible, along with the NBC logo, judges' red Sonic-branded cups, and Simon's Dunkin' Donuts cup.

raw_segments: The video opens with a theatrical stage setting, featuring rich red curtains with golden tassels and hanging Edison-style light bulbs, creating a warm, vintage ambiance. A game show buzzer sounds as the host in a light blue suit stands center stage, animatedly gesturing between two seated guests: the bald man in black suit and the man in dark blue shirt. The host audibly counts down "Three, two, one," followed by a game sound effect and his instruction to put "Hands up!" The bald man in black suit exclaims "What?!" and questions if the host said "Handsome?" The man in dark blue shirt interjects, "That was not, by the way," with the bald man in black suit adding, "This is crazy!" and "I didn't plan that," while the man in dark blue shirt insists, "I swear on my life, that was not prepped." The scene then transitions to a different setting with a vibrant 'America's Got Talent' neon backdrop, where the bald man in black suit and the man in dark blue shirt are now seated in front of the iconic logo, participating in a quiz segment. A female voice, likely from the woman in blue sequined top, asks the first on-screen question: 'I studied what profession for three years?' The man in dark blue shirt confidently states, "I know what it was," and the bald man in black suit answers "Dentistry," explaining, "Because she has amazing teeth. She's gonna be a dentist." The man in dark blue shirt adds a humorous anecdote, "She will actually floss my teeth," leading the bald man in black suit to joke about Sofia Vergara being his dentist. The woman in gray strapless gown audibly reacts, "That sounds so sexy." Another game show sound effect plays as the female voice asks the next question: 'How many Emmys have I been nominated for?' The bald man in black suit guesses "seven," while the man in dark blue shirt suggests "Two Emmys." The woman in white jacket then chimes in, "Seven times. Let's just go for the seven." After another game sound, the man in dark blue shirt asks, "Why are you looking at me like that?" and the bald man in black suit admits, "I'm guessing, I don't know." The female voice reveals the answer "Four," prompting the man in dark blue shirt to protest, "You didn't let me finish," explaining he was going to say "two times two." The bald man in black suit concludes, "Well, she should have got more." The video concludes with a still image of a woman in a white strapless gown posing on the Emmys red carpet, with the NBC and Emmys logos visible in the background, connecting to the quiz question.

event_blocks.summary: Energetic intro music and audience cheers accompanied the opening as Howie Mandel, the bald man in sunglasses, animatedly spoke with both hands from the judging panel, seated in a black jacket over a black shirt with a red Sonic-branded cup and microphone before him, declaring, "The competition was brutal," and reflecting on "the highest of highs and then some incredible for me, personal disappointments" from the previous night, adding, "I am brutally honest. That's why it was brutal. Good luck to everyone." The camera panned back to reveal the full judging panel: Howie, the woman in a sparkly top, Sofia Vergara (the woman in a pink dress), and Simon Cowell, all seated behind a sleek white desk with red Sonic-branded cups and microphones. Sofia, leaning forward and smiling into her microphone, playfully interjected with an accent, "But you sometimes are also grumpy." Howie quickly retorted, "I'm not grumpy," then clarified, "I'm translating. She said grumpy," eliciting audience laughter. Simon, in a dark t-shirt, then stated, "Well, you know, this is a good sign." Terry Crews, the bald host in a shiny gray suit, stood on stage holding a microphone, addressing the judges and audience, asking Sofia, "a lot of the judges, you guys disagreed on a lot of the acts last night. Why do you think that was?" Sofia responded, "first of all, we have different taste," to which Howie immediately interjected, "We all have the same taste." Sofia continued, laughing, "Some of them don't know what they what they what they're supposed to know," prompting Howie to assert, "I know everything we know." Sofia then playfully described Howie as "grumpy sometimes and Simon is sometimes loco," to which Simon, with expressive hand gestures, placed a hand on his chest and admitted, "I admit it," as the audience cheered and laughed in the dimly lit arena with blue and purple stage lighting, creating a vibrant, live show atmosphere, with the NBC 'LIVE' logo displayed in the bottom left corner.

event_blocks.summary: On the America's Got Talent stage, bathed in blue and purple light, the audience cheers as the host, a woman with voluminous curly blonde hair in a black lace dress, introduces "Buzzer Buddy!" She playfully asks Howie, the bald man in sunglasses, what he'd discard from Simon's closet, to which Mel B, the woman in the yellow top, interjects that Simon has "very minimal choices" because "he wears the same thing." Mel B specifies "the white t-shirt and the white dress shirt," or "the bell-bottom pants," and Howie agrees, confirming "the white t-shirt," leading Mel B to exclaim, "The white Simon everything!" Howie concurs, "That is everything," and Mel B awards a point, stating, "I'm gonna give you that point," as the score graphic updates to '2 2' and Simon raises his hand. After asking the audience if they agree, Mel B concludes that's "pretty much all that Simon wears." Heidi, the woman in the sparkly top, then enthusiastically praises the young man in the black shirt with a cross necklace for doing "so much magic" and making her "believe in magic," calling his performance "amazing" and "better than your audition." She eagerly asks Simon's opinion, and as the audience chants "Simon!", Heidi presses, "Did he win you over?" Simon, in a dark t-shirt, calmly replies, "I think I helped you," which Heidi playfully disputes with a "No!", but Simon insists, "I do. I really, really do. I think..." as the segment ends.

The judging panel, including Howie in sunglasses, a woman in a sparkly top, and a woman in a pink dress, reacts with smiles as Simon gestures animatedly. Host Terry, in a shiny dark blue suit, pays tribute to Howie for his 10 years on the show, leading into a countdown from a large screen displaying a cityscape and a billboard celebrating "Happy 10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Howie!" with NBC details. Howie asks "Where am I looking?" before the audience counts down "Five! Four! Three! Two! One!" as lighting shifts from purples and blues to warm stage lights. Later, a wide shot reveals the anniversary billboard again, with a humorous red arrow pointing to an edited image of Howie. Howie laughs heartily, covering his face, as the audience applauds and cheers. The judging panel, including the woman in a sparkly top, the woman in a colorful jacket, and Simon in a white shirt, smiles and laughs, with Simon asking "What? Is that real?" and Howie confirming "Yes!" Simon then embraces Howie amidst continued applause, while the other judges watch with smiles, creating a joyful atmosphere.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Simon, a male judge in a dark t-shirt, speaking expressively with his hand on his chest, his voice acknowledging a performer's improvement: "by me saying no at the auditions, I think has pushed you to be a better magician." He then humorously declares, "I am officially an idiot," eliciting audience cheers. The camera briefly cuts to the young male performer, a magician in a gray collared shirt and cross necklace, smiling confidently on stage under purple lighting. Simon, now in a white shirt, continues his animated reaction, gesturing with both hands into the microphone. The full judging panel, including the bald man in sunglasses (Howie), the woman in a sparkly top, and the woman in a pink dress, react with smiles and engagement as Simon thoughtfully touches his chin. The host, Terry, a bald man in a shiny dark blue suit, takes center stage with a microphone, gesturing expansively. His energetic voice addresses Howie, expressing heartfelt tribute: "what you have done for the show and as a friend, as one of the most amazing people I've ever worked with. I wanted to pay you a tribute to show your significance and most importantly, your importance for the 10 years you've been on the show." Simon then hands over to Terry, saying, "Over to you, Terry," followed by more audience cheers. Terry announces, "It's out on Sunset Boulevard, okay? Let's take a look. Here we go! Count it down with me, people!" as the camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the America's Got Talent stage. A large screen displays a cityscape and a countdown from 04 to 02, then transitions to a billboard reading 'Happy 10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Howie!' with Howie's photo, the AGT logo, and 'TUES & WED NBC'. Howie's voice is heard asking, "Where am I looking?" before Terry leads the audience in a loud countdown: "Five! Four! Three! Two! One!" The dynamic lighting shifts from deep purples and blues to warm stage lights, emphasizing the celebratory and emotional tone of the moment.

The segment opens with an upbeat, celebratory musical fanfare as a wide shot reveals a large outdoor billboard against a twilight sky. The billboard displays a 'Happy 10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Howie!' message, featuring a photo of Howie Mandel, the AGT logo, and 'TUES & WED NBC' text. Humorously, a red arrow points to a small, edited-in image of the bald man in sunglasses (Howie) next to the main photo, with 'HOWIE' written in red marker. As the audience's applause and cheers begin to swell, the scene cuts to the judging panel where the bald man in sunglasses, Howie, is seen laughing heartily, a distinct, almost cackling sound, covering his face with his hand before throwing his head back in amusement. The audience's applause continues, mixed with cheers, as Howie gestures with his hands, clearly delighted and continuing his strong laughter. A wider view of the judging panel shows the woman in sparkly top, the woman in colorful jacket, and the man in white shirt, Simon, all smiling and laughing. Simon is shown laughing broadly, then asks, "What? Is that real?" to which Howie, still laughing, clearly responds, "Yes!" The audience erupts in louder applause and cheers, accompanied by the continuing celebratory music. The billboard is shown again, reinforcing the anniversary message. The scene shifts back to the panel, where Simon stands up and warmly embraces Howie, who reciprocates the hug amidst the ongoing applause. The woman in sparkly top and the woman in colorful jacket watch with smiles, creating a joyful and celebratory atmosphere.
